payload.answer: Create only one memory card. Treat the three deliveries as retries of the same message, not as separate evidence. Keep the claim qualified as reported speech and do not infer stronger or more durable facts than stated.
citations: Input: "Same input delivered three times - Sam from Goldman mentioned that he likes Bill Evans"

entity_resolution.reason: Insufficient information to uniquely resolve Sam, Goldman, or Bill Evans; preserve ambiguity and avoid over-linking.

memory_cards.statement: Sam, described as being from Goldman, mentioned that he likes Bill Evans.

details: Detected repeated identical content and deduplicated it.
Stored a single qualified memory rather than three copies.
Preserved ambiguity around entity identity and affiliation.

repair_options: If desired, rephrase the card to explicitly mark hearsay: "Sam said he likes Bill Evans."
Ask a follow-up to disambiguate identities: which Sam, which Goldman, and which Bill Evans?
If the system tracks retries, attach duplicate-delivery metadata rather than creating new cards.
